message:
chore(artifacts): capture first fp-conformance test_failure_surfaces + per-case evidence jsonl bundle

First on-disk drop of the two artifact trees that the `fp-conformance`
harness writes via `write_failure_surface_jsonl` and
`write_case_evidence_jsonl` (crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs around
line 22520). Neither path is in .gitignore, and the neighboring
`artifacts/phase2c/FP-P2C-*/differential_validation_log.jsonl` files
are already tracked using the same schema — so this is the missing
counterpart evidence for the newer harness outputs.

artifacts/phase2c/test_failure_surfaces/ (43 jsonl files)
  - Per-packet failure surfaces named
    `differential_all_packets_green_FP_P2D_<ID>.jsonl`, emitted when
    the phase2c packet `FP-P2D-<ID>` has at least one non-green case.
    Each line is a `FailureSurfaceEntry`: suite, packet_id, case_id,
    operation, mode, mismatch_class, mismatch detail (with actual vs
    expected values), replay_key, trace_id, and evidence_records.
  - Packet IDs captured so far: FP-P2D-091, 234, 240, 241, 242, 264,
    265, 266, and ~35 others — these are the packets the harness
    flagged as "green overall but with surfaced per-case
    divergences", i.e. exactly the cases future work should bisect.

artifacts/conformance/<packet_id>/<case_id>.jsonl (39 case jsonl
    files under artifacts/conformance/<unknown>/)
  - Per-case evidence written by `write_case_evidence_jsonl`. Each
    line is a `CaseEvidenceEntry` with suite, packet_id, case_id,
    operation, mode (strict / hardened), mismatch_class, mismatch
    detail, replay/trace keys, and the dtype / first-mismatch index
    fields.
  - These are currently bucketed under `<unknown>/` because the
    corresponding packet_id resolution path in the harness returns
    the literal string `"<unknown>"` when the fixture wasn't linked
    to a packet. Covered cases include resample mean/sum basic and
    null variants, rolling mean null, astype str→float, memory_usage
    with nulls, csv parse_dates mixed-timezone strict, and ~30 more.
  - Filename resolution is intentional (sanitize_failure_surface_artifact_name
    strips slashes / whitespace), so the `<unknown>` bucket is a
    stable path for the "unattributed packet" class of evidence
    until the harness teaches itself which packet a given case came
    from — at which point future regen will rewrite into
    artifacts/conformance/FP-P2D-<ID>/<case_id>.jsonl and leave the
    `<unknown>` bucket for genuinely unresolvable cases.

Committing the current bundle snapshots the shape that downstream
audit tooling is already referencing and lets `git diff` show meaningful
case-level churn on future test runs instead of the whole directory
appearing as new-untracked every time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
